mainEntity.name: How should I structure my FAQ content and schema so Google Assistant actually reads my answers in voice search?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To optimize FAQs for voice search, structure each question and answer clearly on the page, then wrap them in FAQ schema that closely mirrors the visible text.

Voice assistants look for direct matches between what’s written and what’s marked up, so consistency and brevity are crucial. Aim for answer‑first content, then apply matching structured data.

Key factors:
- Use one question per FAQ block, no long multi‑part questions
- Keep answers in the 20–60 word range, written in natural language
- Ensure FAQ schema’s question and answer fields match on‑page text exactly
- Avoid stuffing promotional language; focus on helpful, factual responses

Practically, start with your highest‑traffic FAQ page, clean up the content to be concise and conversational, then add FAQ JSON‑LD and test. Once you see good performance, replicate the pattern across other Q&A sections. 

Positioning: If you already have strong FAQ content, schema markup is often the fastest way to translate that into more voice search visibility.
acceptedAnswer.description: Write each FAQ as a single clear question with a concise, conversational answer, then mark it up using FAQ schema where the question and answer fields exactly match your on‑page text. Validate the JSON‑LD, and you’ll greatly increase the odds of those answers being used in voice responses.
